description: Zarr/Icechunk public test datasets collected by @eeholmes for the gridlook visualization project (github discussion #41, converted from issue #26). NOT all Icechunk. Captured verbatim-ish for exploration against GDAL's Zarr and Icechunk drivers. Status reflects gridlook (browser/zarrita) behaviour, NOT GDAL - re-test in GDAL.
caveats: status_group/status are gridlook's results (JS/zarrita/CORS-sensitive); GDAL may differ (no CORS constraint, different codec support).
is_icechunk is a heuristic (format text or .icechunk url or icechunk root layout).
For Icechunk entries, the GDAL connection name is the directory (bare store root); see the companion guide.
source.coop URLs are https (use /vsicurl/); s3:// dynamical/earthmover URLs use /vsis3/ with region + AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST.
source.coop buckets encode the region in the host (e.g. us-west-2.opendata.source.coop) but you STILL must pass --config AWS_REGION us-west-2; GDAL does not parse region from the host.
entries with gdal_verified=true were tested against GDAL's Icechunk driver directly (2026-06-11); others carry gridlook/browser status only.

datasets.title: CHLA-Z
datasets.notes: Loads but very slow and often hangs

datasets.title: dynamical ECMWF AIFS single
datasets.notes: region us-west-2; CONFIRMED in GDAL Icechunk driver

datasets.title: EarthMover ERA5 (spatial group)
datasets.notes: region us-east-1; grouped root; pcodec -> GDAL data reads blocked

datasets.title: ISMIP6 AIS
datasets.status: GDAL: crs FIXED; data blocked on NON-STANDARD numcodecs.* codecs (WONTFIX upstream decision)
datasets.notes: crs 0-D extent FIXED. Data arrays use bytes -> numcodecs.shuffle(elementsize:4) -> numcodecs.zlib(level:3). Even: these are 'unsupported currently by the GDAL Zarr v3 implementation (and are non-standard Zarr v3 codecs)' and 'will not be worked around for now.' numcodecs.* are numcodecs-namespaced EXTENSIONS, not core Zarr v3 codecs (same family as pcodec). Standalone shuffle impl exists but only in the zarr_v2 path; wiring it into v3 would mean GDAL chasing the numcodecs extension namespace - a scope decision declined. This is a STORE-SIDE conformance issue (v2->v3 conversion emitting non-standard codecs), not a GDAL gap. Compare era5_weatherbench2 'wontfix'. Reproducer kept for conformance: /combined/AWI_PISM1/exp02/ligroundf.

datasets.status: will not open; not a grid

datasets.title: CarbonPlan multi-level virtual hybrid icechunk
datasets.notes: region us-west-2; VIRTUAL chunk refs - tests virtual-reference morphing

datasets.title: ISMIP6 AIS (source.coop englacial)
datasets.status: GDAL: crs FIXED; data blocked on NON-STANDARD numcodecs.* codecs (WONTFIX upstream decision)
datasets.notes: crs 0-D extent FIXED. Data arrays use bytes -> numcodecs.shuffle(elementsize:4) -> numcodecs.zlib(level:3). Even: these are 'unsupported currently by the GDAL Zarr v3 implementation (and are non-standard Zarr v3 codecs)' and 'will not be worked around for now.' numcodecs.* are numcodecs-namespaced EXTENSIONS, not core Zarr v3 codecs (same family as pcodec). Standalone shuffle impl exists but only in the zarr_v2 path; wiring it into v3 would mean GDAL chasing the numcodecs extension namespace - a scope decision declined. This is a STORE-SIDE conformance issue (v2->v3 conversion emitting non-standard codecs), not a GDAL gap. Compare era5_weatherbench2 'wontfix'. Reproducer kept for conformance: /combined/AWI_PISM1/exp02/ligroundf.

datasets.title: Antarctica fast flow mask 50m (source.coop englacial demogorgn)
datasets.status: GDAL: WORKS end-to-end (mdim + raster subdataset read)
datasets.notes: CONFIRMED full read in GDAL. fast_flow_mask Int64, 12445x12445, blosc/zstd (shuffle as blosc PARAM, supported). gdal raster info on ZARR:"/vsiicechunk/{...}":/fast_flow_mask gives Origin (-2800225,2800225) PixelSize (450,-450) -> Antarctic Polar Stereographic (EPSG:3031 extent), NoData 0. CURVILINEAR siblings: lat/lon are 2D (y,x); x/y are 1D projected axes. Earlier 403 on subdataset open was SHELL line-wrap dropping --config (NOT a bug) - works on one line. Check whether CRS WKT/EPSG is attached (GeoZarr grid-mapping) vs geotransform-only.

conformance_notes: Icechunk writer skew: 'ic-0.3.16' (no public pip tag) wrote scalar-array manifests[0].extents=[{0,1}] instead of empty extents[]. Recent Icechunk writes empty. A conformance assertion: scalar (0-D) arrays MUST have empty manifest extents[]. GDAL Icechunk driver made tolerant of the non-empty case (2026-06).

CODEC STANDARDS vs ADOPTION (the through-line of this whole investigation). GDAL's Zarr v3 implementation supports CORE v3 codecs only: bytes, blosc, gzip, zstd, crc32c, sharding_indexed. Everything else seen here - pcodec, numcodecs.shuffle, numcodecs.zlib, numcodecs.delta, ... - is a NON-CORE codec declared via the numcodecs-zarr3 compatibility shim (zarr.codecs.numcodecs). Three facts hold simultaneously, keep them distinct: (1) SPEC STATUS: these are non-core/non-standard Zarr v3 codecs. (2) READER BEHAVIOUR: a core-only reader (current GDAL v3) will not decode them and reports 'Unsupported codec' / 'codec not available'; this is CORRECT w.r.t. the spec. Even Rouault declined to work around them for GDAL for now, even where a compatible impl exists in the v2 path (shuffle) - out of scope. (3) ADOPTION: some of these - pcodec especially - are in HEAVY real-world use in the xarray/zarr community (e.g. Earthmover ERA5). Non-standard does NOT mean illegitimate or rare. PRACTICAL CONSEQUENCE: stores using non-core codecs are readable, just not by a core-spec reader. They belong to numcodecs-aware readers: zarr-python, or Rust readers built with the codec feature (zarrs/zr lineage, which has a pcodec feature). This is exactly the GDAL/vapour path vs Rust/zr path split: pcodec's popularity is WHY the Rust-backed path matters. SEVERITIES within the non-core family: numcodecs.shuffle is non-core BUT a compatible impl exists in GDAL's v2 path (one barrier: scope). pcodec is non-core AND unimplemented in GDAL (two barriers: scope + no algorithm). Both are unsupported while the 'no non-standard codecs' scope decision stands. CONFORMANCE ASSERTION for a shared suite: a store targeting maximal interoperability should use core v3 codecs; v2->v3 conversion that emits numcodecs.* yields stores that core-spec readers cannot read - a known, documentable trade-off, not a reader bug.
